words.def: an area of activity, interest or knowledge
words.example: in the realm of something, in the realm of literature

words.def: at or near the back of something
words.example: front and rear windows

words.def: the process of thinking about things in a logical way; opinions and ideas that are based on logical thinking
words.example: What is the reasoning behind this decision?

words.def: to say or do something that makes somebody less frightened or worried
words.example: reassure somebody (about something), They tried to reassure her, but she still felt anxious.

words.def: a person who fights against the government of their country
words.example: rebel forces

words.def: an attempt by some of the people in a country to change their government, using violence
words.example: in rebellion (against somebody/something), The north of the country rose in rebellion against the government.

words.def: a person who receives something
words.example: recipients of awards

words.def: the process of changing or improving the condition of something or the way it works; the process of putting something back into the state it was in before
words.example: the reconstruction of the educational system

words.def: sound or pictures that have been recorded on computer files or on CD, DVD, video, etc.
words.example: an audio recording

words.def: an occasion when all the people of a country can vote on an important issue
words.example: referendum on something, Switzerland decided to hold a referendum on joining the EU.

words.def: an image in a mirror, on a shiny surface, on water, etc.
words.example: He admired his reflection in the mirror.

words.def: change that is made to a social system, an organization, etc. in order to improve or correct it
words.example: a government committed to reform

words.def: shelter or protection from danger, trouble, etc.
words.example: A further 300 people have taken refuge in the US embassy.

words.def: an act of saying or showing that you will not do, give or accept something
words.example: the refusal of a request/an invitation/an offer

words.def: to get back something you no longer have, especially an ability or a quality
words.example: I struggled to regain some dignity.

words.def: paying no attention, even if the situation is bad or there are difficulties
words.example: The weather was terrible but we carried on regardless.

words.def: a method or system of government, especially one that has not been elected in a fair way
words.example: a fascist/totalitarian/military, etc. regime

words.def: a person or an organization that officially controls an area of business or industry and makes sure that it is operating fairly
words.example: Ofgas, the gas industry regulator

words.def: having the power to control an area of business or industry and make sure that it is operating fairly
words.example: regulatory bodies/authorities/agencies

words.def: the process of helping somebody to return to a normal, healthy life after they have been in prison or very ill
words.example: a drug rehabilitation centre

words.def: the period during which a king, queen, emperor, etc. rules
words.example: By the end of his reign, the vast empire was in decline.

words.def: the act of refusing to accept or consider something
words.example: Her proposal met with unanimous rejection.

words.def: a close connection with the subject you are discussing or the situation you are in
words.example: I don't see the relevance of your question.

words.def: the quality of being able to be trusted to do what somebody wants or needs
words.example: The incident cast doubt on her motives and reliability.

words.def: hesitating before doing something because you do not want to do it or because you are not sure that it is the right thing to do
words.example: He finally gave a reluctant smile.

words.def: the people, things or time that remain
words.example: I kept some of his books and gave away the remainder.

words.def: the parts of something that are left after the other parts have been used, eaten, removed, etc.
words.example: She fed the remains of her lunch to the dog.

words.def: a way of dealing with or improving an unpleasant or difficult situation
words.example: When the reservoir becomes blocked, the only remedy lies in cleaning the entire system.

words.def: something that makes you think about or remember somebody/something, that you have forgotten or would like to forget
words.example: reminder of something, The sheer size of the cathedral is a constant reminder of the power of religion.

words.def: the act of taking somebody/something away from a particular place
words.example: Clearance of the site required the removal of a number of trees.

words.def: to cause somebody/something to be in a particular state or condition
words.example: to render something harmless/useless/ineffective

words.def: to begin something again after it stopped or was interrupted
words.example: The army renewed its assault on the capital.

words.def: famous and respected
words.example: a renowned author

words.def: the amount of money that you pay to use something for a particular period of time
words.example: Telephone charges include line rental.

words.def: the act of replacing one thing with another, especially something that is newer or better
words.example: the replacement of worn car parts

words.def: according to what some people say
words.example: The band have reportedly decided to split up.

words.def: the act of presenting somebody/something in a particular way; something that shows or describes something
words.example: the negative representation of single mothers in the media

words.def: to make a copy of a picture, piece of text, etc.
words.example: It is illegal to reproduce these worksheets without permission from the publisher.

words.def: the act or process of producing babies, young animals or plants
words.example: sexual reproduction

words.def: a country that is governed by a president and politicians elected by the people and where there is no king or queen
words.example: newly independent republics

words.def: to look like or be similar to another person or thing
words.example: She closely resembles her sister.

words.def: to live in a particular place
words.example: He returned to Britain in 1939, having resided abroad for many years.

words.def: a house, especially a large or impressive one
words.example: a desirable family residence for sale (= for example, in an advertisement)

words.def: suitable for living in; consisting of houses rather than factories or offices
words.example: a quiet residential area

words.def: a small amount of something that remains at the end of a process
words.example: pesticide residues in fruit and vegetables

words.def: the act of giving up your job or position; the occasion when you do this
words.example: a letter of resignation

words.def: dislike of or opposition to a plan, an idea, etc.; the act of refusing to obey
words.example: As with all new ideas it met with resistance.

words.def: belonging or relating separately to each of the people or things already mentioned
words.example: They are each recognized specialists in their respective fields.

words.def: in the same order as the people or things already mentioned
words.example: Julie and Mark, aged 17 and 19 respectively

words.def: the work of repairing and cleaning an old building, a painting, etc. so that its condition is as good as it originally was
words.example: restoration work

words.def: a rule, a fact, an idea, etc. that limits or controls what people can do
words.example: The government has imposed export restraints on some products.

words.def: if you resume an activity, or if it resumes, it begins again or continues after being interrupted
words.example: resume (something), to resume talks/negotiations

words.def: a movement away from a place or an enemy because of danger or defeat
words.example: Napoleon’s retreat from Moscow

words.def: to bring or get something back, especially from a place where it should not be
words.example: retrieve something from somebody/something, She bent to retrieve her comb from the floor.

words.def: a fact that people are made aware of, especially one that has been secret and is surprising
words.example: revelation about/concerning something, startling/sensational revelations about her private life

words.def: something that you do in order to make somebody suffer because they have made you suffer
words.example: revenge for something, She is seeking revenge for the murder of her husband.

words.def: opposite to what has been mentioned
words.example: to travel in the reverse direction

words.def: an improvement in the condition or strength of something
words.example: the revival of trade

words.def: to become, or to make somebody/something become, conscious or healthy and strong again
words.example: The flowers soon revived in water.

words.def: connected with political revolution
words.example: a revolutionary leader

words.def: speech or writing that is intended to influence people, but that is not completely honest or sincere
words.example: the rhetoric of political slogans

words.def: a gun with a long barrel which you hold to your shoulder to fire
words.example: a hunting rifle

words.def: a situation in which a group of people behave in a violent way in a public place, often as a protest
words.example: One prison guard was killed when a riot broke out in the jail.

words.def: to tear something or to become torn, often suddenly or violently
words.example: rip (something), I ripped my jeans on the fence.

words.def: a series of actions that are always performed in the same way, especially as part of a religious ceremony
words.example: religious rituals

words.def: strong and healthy
words.example: She was almost 90, but still very robust.

words.def: the hard solid material that forms part of the surface of the earth and some other planets
words.example: They drilled through several layers of rock to reach the oil.

words.def: a long straight piece of wood, metal or glass
words.example: The concrete is reinforced with steel rods.

words.def: to move or turn around a central fixed point; to make something do this
words.example: Stay well away from the helicopter when its blades start to rotate.

words.def: the action of an object moving in a circle around a central fixed point
words.example: the daily rotation of the earth on its axis

words.def: an official decision made by somebody in a position of authority, especially a judge
words.example: The court will make its ruling on the case next week.

words.def: a piece of information, or a story, that people talk about, but that may not be true
words.example: to start/spread a rumour

words.def: to dismiss somebody from a job
words.example: She was sacked for refusing to work on Sundays.

words.def: connected with God or a god; considered to be holy
words.example: a sacred image/shrine/temple

words.def: the fact of giving up something important or valuable to you in order to get or do something that seems more important; something that you give up in this way
words.example: The makers of the product assured us that there had been no sacrifice of quality.

words.def: a person that the Christian Church recognizes as being very holy, because of the way they have lived or died
words.example: St John

words.def: an official order that limits trade, contact, etc. with a particular country, in order to make it do something, such as obeying international law
words.example: Trade sanctions were imposed against any country that refused to sign the agreement.

words.def: the right to influence something by giving your opinion before a decision is made
words.example: say (in something), We had no say in the decision to sell the company.

words.def: spread far apart over a wide area or over a long period of time
words.example: a few scattered settlements

words.def: having doubts that a claim or statement is true or that something will happen
words.example: She looked highly sceptical.

words.def: the opportunity or ability to do or achieve something
words.example: scope for something, There's still plenty of scope for improvement.

words.def: a thin pointed piece of metal like a nail with a raised spiral line (called a thread) along it and a line or cross cut into its head. Screws are turned and pressed into wood, metal, etc. with a screwdriver in order to fasten two things together.
words.example: One of the screws is loose.

words.def: careful and complete examination
words.example: Her argument doesn't really stand up to scrutiny.

words.def: an official design or mark, stamped on a document to show that it is real and carries the authority of a particular person or organization
words.example: The letter bore the president's seal.

words.def: not connected with spiritual or religious matters
words.example: secular music

words.def: in a way that appears to be true but may in fact not be
words.example: a seemingly stupid question

words.def: a part of something that is separate from the other parts or can be considered separately
words.example: She cleaned a small segment of the painting.

words.def: to take somebody/something in your hand suddenly and using force
words.example: seize something from somebody, She tried to seize the gun from him.

words.def: not often
words.example: He had seldom seen a child with so much talent.

words.def: affecting or involving only a small number of people or things from a larger group
words.example: the selective breeding of cattle

words.def: a member of a senate
words.example: Senator McCarthy

words.def: a feeling that you get when something affects your body
words.example: a tingling/burning sensation

words.def: the ability to understand other people’s feelings
words.example: sensitivity to the needs of children

words.def: a feeling or an opinion, especially one based on emotions
words.example: the spread of nationalist sentiments

words.def: the act of separating people or things; the state of being separate
words.example: separation from somebody/something, the state’s eventual separation from the federation

words.def: doing the same thing in the same way several times; done in the same way several times
words.example: a serial rapist

words.def: a way of organizing something; a system
words.example: I've only been here a couple of weeks and I don't really know the set-up.

words.def: an official agreement that ends an argument between two people or groups
words.example: to negotiate a peace settlement

words.def: the feelings and activities connected with a person’s sexual desires
words.example: He was confused about his sexuality.

words.def: an owner of shares in a company or business
words.example: the major shareholders in the company

words.def: to suddenly break into small pieces; to make something suddenly break into small pieces
words.example: shatter (into something), He dropped the vase and it shattered into pieces on the floor.

words.def: to get rid of something that is no longer wanted
words.example: The factory is shedding a large number of jobs.

words.def: used to emphasize the size, degree or amount of something
words.example: The area is under threat from the sheer number of tourists using it.

words.def: ships in general or considered as a group
words.example: The canal is open to shipping.

words.def: the part that grows up from the ground when a plant starts to grow; a new part that grows on plants or trees
words.example: new green shoots

words.def: to become smaller, especially when washed in water that is too hot; to make clothes, cloth, etc. smaller in this way
words.example: My sweater shrank in the wash.

words.def: to raise your shoulders and then drop them to show that you do not know or care about something
words.example: Sam shrugged and said nothing.

words.def: an act or the sound of sighing
words.example: to give/heave/let out a sigh

words.def: to create particular conditions that exist in real life using computers, models, etc., usually for study or training purposes
words.example: Computer software can be used to simulate conditions on the seabed.

words.def: a situation in which a particular set of conditions is created artificially in order to study or experience something that could exist in reality
words.example: a computer simulation of how the planet functions

words.def: at the same time as something else
words.example: The game will be broadcast simultaneously on TV and radio.

words.def: an offence against God or against a religious or moral law
words.example: to commit a sin

words.def: in a particular place or position
words.example: My bedroom was situated on the top floor of the house.

words.def: a simple picture that is drawn quickly and does not have many details
words.example: The artist is making sketches for his next painting.

words.def: to move forwards lightly and quickly making a little jump with each step
words.example: She skipped happily along beside me.

words.def: to shut, or to make something shut, with a lot of force, making a loud noise
words.example: I heard the door slam behind him.

words.def: to hit somebody/something with the flat part of your hand
words.example: She slapped his face hard.

words.def: to make a long cut with a sharp object, especially in a violent way
words.example: slash something, Someone had slashed the tyres on my car.

words.def: the state of being forced to work as a slave
words.example: to be sold into slavery
